emergent_themes.description: Students are aware that subscriptions make them order more but continue using them anyway, showing sophisticated understanding of behavioral economics
Explicit acknowledgment of laziness as a primary driver, without shame or justification - purely transactional relationship with convenience
Regular comparison shopping across platforms, treating apps as interchangeable tools rather than brands with loyalty

coding_summary: P011 represents the archetypal convenience-maximizer student who has fully embraced delivery as life infrastructure. Key findings include: (1) Explicit laziness as primary trigger without shame, (2) Sophisticated understanding of subscription economics while still being influenced by them, (3) Systematic price comparison across platforms, (4) Complete individualization of food behavior even in shared housing, (5) Pragmatic approach to 'cooking' using convenience foods, (6) Post-graduation expectations of increased delivery usage. Strong coverage across most ROs with particularly rich data on delivery triggers and subscription behavior.

coverage_gaps.reason: Participant shows no stigma around solo ordering - treats it as completely normal and practical
